item.title: Asian operator begins chase for specialised offshore platform at flagship asset
item.description: New conductor supported platform will be a six-slot unit, located about 1.75 kilometres from ONGC's existing NQO process platform

item.title: Nabors sees daily rig average rise thanks to boost in US activity
item.description: Drilling contractor posts 6% jump in first quarter revenue

item.title: Expand Energy hits projections, reveals new LNG deal in first quarter report
item.description: US gas producer strikes new agreement with Delfin LNG project in US Gulf

item.title: Eni strikes deals to ramp up Venezuela production
item.description: Italian major agrees to increase output at Junin-5 in Orinoco Belt and at Perla offshore gas field

item.title: BP eyes upstream growth beyond blockbuster trading results
item.description: Trading profits in the first quarter were almost double the prior-year-period

item.title: London-listed junior makes foray into Gabon
item.description: Block's chief executive Paul Haywood described the transaction as “an excellent value-accretive acquisition”

item.title: UAE to exit Opec and Opec+ amid continued disruption at Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Country decides to leave Opec producers’ group as it plans for “accelerated investment in domestic energy production”, UAE’s Ministry of Energy & Infrastructure said

item.title: Petrobras buys Campos basin field from Shell and partners for $290 million
item.description: Intricate deal involves a portion of the shared pre-salt Jubarte reservoir

item.title: Petrobras launches tender to charter flotel for work offshore Brazil
item.description: Contract on offer could be worth approximately $200 million

item.title: Tullow completes refinance deal; share price edges up
item.description: Tullow gets two-years of breathing room to address financial burden

item.title: Murphy dominates Cameroon licensing round
item.description: US indie picks up for blocks while UK-based minnow lands a single tract

item.title: Iraq picks new PM as nation's oil exports struggle amid Middle East war
item.description: Iraq has lost the ability to export close to 80% of oil production after US-Israel war in Iran shut down Strait of Hormuz

item.title: Ghana talks with potential partners over billion-barrel onshore block
item.description: State-owned GNPC Explorco could drill as many as four wells in a Voltaian basin block that is attracting farm-in interest

item.title: Adnoc gets first laden LNG carrier out of Gulf
item.description: The Mubaraz looks to have become only the second LNG vessel to exit the Strait of Hormuz since war broke out

item.title: BP beats expectations as oil trading and upstream offset lower gas earnings in first quarter
item.description: Supermajor's earnings came in comfortably ahead of analyst forecasts

item.title: UAE’s largest offshore yard hit sees net profit drop 63% on ‘cost overruns’ and logistics challenges
item.description: NMDC Energy is a key offshore contractor for the state giant Adnoc, which is embarking on a major investment drive to boost its oil production capacity to 5 million bpd

item.title: Queensland seeks to accelerate development of Australia’s 'new oil province'
item.description: Shell, Omega, Elixir and Santos progressing with exploration activities in Taroom Trough

item.title: Chinese contractor wins $4.6 billion Turkmenistan gas field contract
item.description: Natural gas processing plant to be built for the Galkynysh natural gas field

item.title: $12 billion Middle East gas megaproject to spark major contractor interest
item.description: Aramco chief executive Amin Nasser revealed late last year that the company had revised its previous 2030 target to increase gas production by 60% from 2021 levels to 80%

item.title: US steers another $885 million from offshore wind to oil and gas
item.description: Two more offshore wind developers strike deal with Trump to cancel plans and invest in US fossil fuels

item.title: New deepwater appetite: Iran war boosts demand for offshore rigs, says Noble
item.description: The Middle East war is igniting global interest in deepwater drilling, the company says

item.title: Halliburton wraps up Greenland frontier exploration deal
item.description: Greenland Energy targets two-well onshore drilling campaign this year

item.title: Shell strikes $16.4 billion deal to buy Canadian player
item.description: Operator of LNG Canada lands agreement to acquire Arc Resources, establishing Canada as new 'heartland'

item.title: Chinese offshore giant makes start on latest carbon capture project
item.description: CNOOC Ltd has reportedly started construction on Dongfang 1-1 CCUS

item.title: Iraq expedites pipeline plan amid Strait of Hormuz disruption
item.description: Iraqi Prime Minister approves funding and appoints body to oversee Basra-Haditha pipeline project

item.title: Operator drops CCS exploration permit in Norway
item.description: Partners in the proposed Iroko project relinquish offshore licence

item.title: Eni exploration chief picks out key wells for rest of 2026
item.description: Wells targeting 'material' resources will be drilled in Indonesia, Ghana and Egypt

item.title: Iraq's oil strategy must be built for disruption, not stability
item.description: Venandi founder and analyst Howar Talabany looks at Iraq's oil export options outside the Strait of Hormuz

item.title: Shell’s flagship Dutch green hydrogen project is nearly complete. Can it be replicated across Europe?
item.description: Supermajor estimates around 500 similar-sized projects would be needed to meet the EU’s 2030 production target, but that it may not be as simple as a copy-paste approach

item.title: Flag day: Drillship fixed for rare Ghana deepwater exploration well
item.description: Dubai-based conglomerate set to spud probe in late 2026
